    Qwen3.5 stores this inside ``rope_parameters`` rather than at top level.
    """
    prf = llm_dict.get("partial_rotary_factor")
    if prf is not None:
        return float(prf)
    for key in ("rope_parameters", "rope_scaling"):
        nested = llm_dict.get(key)
        if not isinstance(nested, dict):
            continue
        if nested.get("partial_rotary_factor") is not None:
            return float(nested["partial_rotary_factor"])
        for attention_type in ("full_attention", "sliding_attention"):
            attention_params = nested.get(attention_type)
            if (isinstance(attention_params, dict) and
                    attention_params.get("partial_rotary_factor") is not None):
                return float(attention_params["partial_rotary_factor"])
    return 1.0


def _detect_has_qk_norm(model_dir: str) -> bool:
    """Detect QK-norm by scanning checkpoint key names for ``.q_norm.weight``.

    This is model-agnostic: any architecture that stores per-head Q/K norms
    as ``*.q_norm.weight`` buffers will be detected correctly.
    """
    return any(".q_norm.weight" in k
               for k in _checkpoint_weight_keys(model_dir))


def _checkpoint_weight_keys(model_dir: str) -> List[str]:
    """Return checkpoint tensor keys, ignoring stale shard indexes when needed."""
    index_path = os.path.join(model_dir, "model.safetensors.index.json")
    single_path = os.path.join(model_dir, "model.safetensors")
    if os.path.exists(index_path):
        try:
            with open(index_path) as f:
                index = json.load(f)
            weight_map = index.get("weight_map", {})
            missing_shards = {
                shard
                for shard in set(weight_map.values())
                if not os.path.exists(os.path.join(model_dir, shard))
            }
            if not missing_shards or not os.path.exists(single_path):
                return list(weight_map.keys())
        except (OSError, json.JSONDecodeError):
            pass

    if os.path.exists(single_path):
        try:
            from safetensors import safe_open
            with safe_open(single_path, framework="pt") as f:
                return list(f.keys())
        except (OSError, ImportError):
            pass
    return []


_VL_LLM_PREFIXES = ("language_model.", "text_model.", "llm.", "thinker.")


def _strip_vl_prefix(name: str) -> str:
    """Strip a known VL wrapper prefix (e.g. ``language_model.``) if present."""
    if name.startswith("model.language_model."):
        return "model." + name[len("model.language_model."):]
    if name.startswith("model.visual."):
        return "visual." + name[len("model.visual."):]
    for prefix in _VL_LLM_PREFIXES:
        if name.startswith(prefix):
            return name[len(prefix):]
    return name


def _normalize_module_name(name: str) -> str:
    """Normalise a checkpoint / hf_quant_config module name to the short
    namespace that ``make_linear`` uses (``layers.N...``, ``lm_head``, etc.).

    LLM ``make_linear`` callers pass names without any ``model.`` prefix
    (see ``modeling_default.py``: ``module_name=f"layers.{i}.mlp.gate_proj"``).
    For multimodal checkpoints whose keys are
    ``model.language_model.layers.N...`` the entire compound prefix must be
    stripped so the resulting short name matches what ``module_quant_type``
    looks up.  Single VL prefixes and bare ``model.`` follow the same rule.

    Used by ``_effective_excluded_modules``, ``_detect_modelopt_unquantized_linears``,
    and ``_parse_mixed_precision`` so that ``excluded``, ``layer_overrides``,
    and ``module_name`` all share the same name space.
    """
    if name.startswith("model.language_model."):
        return name[len("model.language_model."):]
    if name.startswith("thinker.model."):
        return name[len("thinker.model."):]
    for prefix in _VL_LLM_PREFIXES + ("model.", ):
        if name.startswith(prefix):
            return name[len(prefix):]
    return name


def _detect_unquantized_modules(model_dir: str) -> List[str]:
    """Return module names whose weights are plain float (not int4 quantized).

    Some layers (often ``lm_head``) use ``*.weight`` instead of ``*.qweight``.
    Checkpoint wrapper prefixes (``model.``, ``language_model.``, etc.) are
    stripped so that the returned names match the short names used by
    ``make_linear()``.
    """
    all_keys = _checkpoint_weight_keys(model_dir)

    # Top-level module prefix = everything before the last dot segment
    qweight_modules = {
        k.rsplit(".", 1)[0]
        for k in all_keys if k.endswith(".qweight")
    }
    weight_modules = {
        k.rsplit(".", 1)[0]
        for k in all_keys if k.endswith(".weight")
    }
    excluded: List[str] = [
        _normalize_module_name(m) for m in (weight_modules - qweight_modules)
    ]
    # lm_head may have neither .weight nor .qweight when tie_word_embeddings=True
    # (the checkpoint omits lm_head.weight entirely).  Treat it as FP16 so that
    # tie_weights() can clone embed_tokens.weight into it after loading.
    all_linear_stripped = {
        _normalize_module_name(m)
        for m in qweight_modules | weight_modules
    }
    if "lm_head" not in all_linear_stripped:
        excluded.append("lm_head")
    return _with_gdn_fused_exclusions(excluded)


_GDN_INPUT_PROJ_MODULES = ("in_proj_qkv", "in_proj_z", "in_proj_b",
                           "in_proj_a")


def _with_gdn_fused_exclusions(modules: List[str]) -> List[str]:
    """Add synthetic GDN fused projections when all source projections are FP16."""
    result = set(modules)
    by_prefix: Dict[str, set] = {}
    for module in result:
        for proj in _GDN_INPUT_PROJ_MODULES:
            suffix = f".{proj}"
            if module.endswith(suffix):
                by_prefix.setdefault(module[:-len(suffix)], set()).add(proj)
                break
    for prefix, projs in by_prefix.items():
        if all(proj in projs for proj in _GDN_INPUT_PROJ_MODULES):
            result.add(f"{prefix}.in_proj_fused")
    return sorted(result)


def _detect_quantized_modules(model_dir: str) -> List[str]:
    """Return modules that have checkpoint quantization sidecars."""
    all_keys = _checkpoint_weight_keys(model_dir)

    suffixes = (".qweight", ".weight_scale", ".weight_scale_2", ".input_scale",
                ".scales")
    modules = {
        _strip_vl_prefix(k.rsplit(".", 1)[0])
        for k in all_keys if k.endswith(suffixes)
    }
    return sorted(modules)


def _effective_excluded_modules(model_dir: str,
                                excluded: List[str]) -> List[str]:
    """Drop exclusions contradicted by quantized tensors in the checkpoint,
    and normalize remaining names so they match what ``make_linear`` looks up.

    Normalisation mirrors ``_parse_mixed_precision``'s strip on
    ``layer_overrides`` keys (``language_model.`` / ``text_model.`` / ``llm.``
    / ``model.``) so a checkpoint that writes ``model.visual.blocks.X.Y`` to
    ``exclude_modules`` matches the ``visual.blocks.X.Y`` ``module_name`` the
    modeling code passes.
    """
    quantized_modules = set(_detect_quantized_modules(model_dir))
    normalized = [
        _normalize_module_name(module) for module in excluded
        if _strip_vl_prefix(module) not in quantized_modules
    ]
    return _with_gdn_fused_exclusions(normalized)


def _detect_modelopt_unquantized_linears(model_dir: str) -> List[str]:
    """Return module_name strings of Linears the ModelOpt checkpoint left unquantized.

    A ModelOpt-quantized Linear stores both ``<name>.weight`` (packed) and
    ``<name>.weight_scale`` (scale tensor; FP8 / NVFP4 / MXFP8 / AWQ / INT8-SQ
    all emit this).  Linears that ModelOpt skipped — typically because their
    wildcard had ``enable: False`` (visual / audio / lm_head) — only have
    ``<name>.weight``.

    We compute the set of "has .weight without .weight_scale" modules from the
    checkpoint index, then return them in the short form ``make_linear`` uses
    (leading ``model.`` and VL wrapper prefixes stripped).  Norm and embedding
    names also fall into this set but are harmless: ``make_linear`` only
    consults ``excluded`` for paths that actually go through it (i.e. real
    Linears), so extra entries are inert.

    Used by ``_parse_quant`` to plug a long-standing gap: for dominant-quant
    checkpoints (``quant_algo: FP8 / NVFP4 / W4A16_AWQ / ...``) ModelOpt does
    NOT populate ``exclude_modules`` even when whole submodules (visual tower,
    audio encoder) were skipped during PTQ.  Without this augmentation,
    ``make_linear``'s dominant fallback would build NVFP4Linear / FP8Linear
    against FP16 weights → shape mismatch on export.

    Complements ``_effective_excluded_modules`` (which drops false-positive
    excludes when the checkpoint contradicts them).  This helper supplies the
    opposite direction: false-negative excludes when ``exclude_modules`` is
    silent on a submodule that was actually skipped during PTQ.
    """
    all_keys = _checkpoint_weight_keys(model_dir)

    if not all_keys:
        return []

    weight_modules = {
        k.rsplit(".", 1)[0]
        for k in all_keys if k.endswith(".weight")
    }
    scale_modules = {
        k.rsplit(".", 1)[0]
        for k in all_keys if k.endswith(".weight_scale")
    }
    unquantized = weight_modules - scale_modules

    excluded = set()
    for name in unquantized:
        normalized = _normalize_module_name(name)
        if normalized.endswith(".self_attn.qkv_proj"):
            prefix = normalized[:-len("qkv_proj")]
            excluded.update(f"{prefix}{proj}"
                            for proj in ("q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj"))
        elif normalized.endswith(".mlp.gate_up_proj"):
            prefix = normalized[:-len("gate_up_proj")]
            excluded.update(f"{prefix}{proj}"
                            for proj in ("gate_proj", "up_proj"))
        else:
            excluded.add(normalized)

    # Normalise to the same name space ``layer_overrides`` and ``excluded``
    # use, so ``make_linear`` finds entries via its ``module_name`` lookup.
    return _with_gdn_fused_exclusions(sorted(excluded))


def _parse_quant(model_dir: str, config: dict) -> QuantConfig:
    """Determine quantisation config from hf_quant_config.json or config.json."""

    # ---- Sidecar hf_quant_config.json ---------------------------------------
    hf_path = os.path.join(model_dir, "hf_quant_config.json")
    if os.path.exists(hf_path):
        with open(hf_path) as f:
            hq = json.load(f)
        q = hq.get("quantization", {})
        algo = (q.get("quant_algo") or "").upper()
        if "AWQ" in algo and "W4A16" in algo:
            # Drop exclusions that the checkpoint contradicts (false positives),
            # then augment with submodules ModelOpt actually left unquantized
            # (false negatives — typically visual tower / audio encoder).
            excluded = _effective_excluded_modules(
                model_dir, list(q.get("exclude_modules", [])))
            excluded.extend(
                m for m in _detect_modelopt_unquantized_linears(model_dir)
                if m not in excluded)
            return QuantConfig(
                quant_type=QUANT_INT4_AWQ_MODELOPT,
                group_size=int(q.get("group_size", 128)),
                excluded=excluded,
            )
        if algo == "MIXED_PRECISION":
            quantized_layers = q.get("quantized_layers", {})
            dominant, group_size, layer_overrides = _parse_mixed_precision(
                quantized_layers)
            return QuantConfig(
                quant_type=dominant,
                group_size=group_size,
                kv_cache_quant=_kv_norm(q.get("kv_cache_quant_algo", "")),
                excluded=_effective_excluded_modules(
                    model_dir, list(q.get("exclude_modules", []))),
                layer_overrides=layer_overrides,
                is_mixed_precision=True,
            )
        qt = _algo_to_quant_type(algo)
        gs = int(q.get("group_size", 1))
        if qt == QUANT_MXFP8 and gs == 1:
            gs = 32  # MXFP8 default block_size
        excluded = _effective_excluded_modules(
            model_dir, list(q.get("exclude_modules", [])))
        excluded.extend(
            m for m in _detect_modelopt_unquantized_linears(model_dir)
            if m not in excluded)
        return QuantConfig(
            quant_type=qt,
            group_size=gs,
            kv_cache_quant=_kv_norm(q.get("kv_cache_quant_algo", "")),
            excluded=excluded,
        )

    # ---- Embedded quantization_config in config.json ------------------------
    qc = config.get("quantization_config")
    if qc is None:
        return QuantConfig()

    # Embedded block with ``quant_algo`` (export tool formats)
    if "quant_algo" in qc:
        algo = (qc.get("quant_algo") or "").upper()
        if "W4A16" in algo and "AWQ" in algo:
            return QuantConfig(
                quant_type=QUANT_INT4_AWQ_MODELOPT,
                group_size=int(qc.get("group_size", 128)),
                excluded=_effective_excluded_modules(
                    model_dir, list(qc.get("ignore", []))),
            )
        group_size = 1
        cg = qc.get("config_groups", {})
        if cg:
            first_group = next(iter(cg.values()), {})
            group_size = int(
                first_group.get("weights", {}).get("group_size", 1))
        kv = qc.get("kv_cache_scheme")
        kv_str = "fp8" if kv else None
        return QuantConfig(
            quant_type=_algo_to_quant_type(algo),
            group_size=group_size,
            kv_cache_quant=kv_str,
            excluded=_effective_excluded_modules(model_dir,
                                                 list(qc.get("ignore", []))),
        )

    # quant_method == awq (column-packed int4 checkpoints)
    if qc.get("quant_method") == "awq":
        return QuantConfig(
            quant_type=QUANT_INT4_AWQ,
            group_size=int(qc.get("group_size", 128)),
            excluded=_detect_unquantized_modules(model_dir),
        )

    # quant_method == gptq
    if qc.get("quant_method") == "gptq":
        return QuantConfig(
            quant_type=QUANT_INT4_GPTQ,
            group_size=int(qc.get("group_size", 128)),
            gptq_zero_point_offset=_detect_gptq_zero_point_offset(
                model_dir, qc),
            excluded=_detect_unquantized_modules(model_dir),
        )

    return QuantConfig()


def _detect_gptq_zero_point_offset(model_dir: str, qc: dict) -> int:
    """Infer whether GPTQ qzeros stores ``zero`` or ``zero - 1``.

    Older symmetric GPTQ checkpoints used by Qwen3 store packed ``0x77777777``
    for a real zero point of 8.  Qwen3.5 stores packed ``0x88888888`` for the
    same real zero point.  Inspecting a tiny qzeros sample lets both variants
    share the same repacking path without depending on GPTQModel/Optimum.
    """
    if not bool(qc.get("sym", False)):
        return 1

    try:
        from safetensors import safe_open

        index_path = os.path.join(model_dir, "model.safetensors.index.json")
        if os.path.exists(index_path):
            with open(index_path) as f:
                weight_map: dict = json.load(f).get("weight_map", {})
            qzeros_key = next((k for k in weight_map if k.endswith(".qzeros")),
                              None)
            if qzeros_key is None:
                return 1
            shard_path = os.path.join(model_dir, weight_map[qzeros_key])
        else:
            shard_path = os.path.join(model_dir, "model.safetensors")
            if not os.path.exists(shard_path):
                return 1
            with safe_open(shard_path, framework="pt") as f:
                qzeros_key = next(
                    (k for k in f.keys() if k.endswith(".qzeros")), None)
            if qzeros_key is None:
                return 1

        with safe_open(shard_path, framework="pt") as f:
            qzeros = f.get_tensor(qzeros_key).flatten()[:1024].cpu().tolist()
    except Exception:
        return 1

    if not qzeros:
        return 1

    nibbles = []
    for value in qzeros:
        packed = int(value) & 0xFFFFFFFF
        nibbles.extend((packed >> (4 * i)) & 0xF for i in range(8))

    if nibbles and all(v == 8 for v in nibbles):
        return 0
    return 1


def _algo_to_quant_type(algo: str) -> str:
    algo = algo.upper()
    # MXFP8 per-block must be checked before generic FP8
    if "FP8_PB" in algo or "MXFP8" in algo:
        return QUANT_MXFP8
    if "FP8" in algo:
        return QUANT_FP8
    if "FP4" in algo or "NVFP4" in algo:
        return QUANT_NVFP4
    # W4A16_AWQ from ModelOpt unified checkpoints uses prepacked uint8 weights;
    # plain AWQ / INT4_AWQ from HuggingFace uses column-packed int32 qweight.
    if "W4A16" in algo and "AWQ" in algo:
        return QUANT_INT4_AWQ_MODELOPT
    if "AWQ" in algo or "INT4_AWQ" in algo:
        return QUANT_INT4_AWQ
    if "W8A8" in algo or "INT8" in algo:
        return QUANT_INT8_SQ
    return QUANT_FP16


def _parse_mixed_precision(quantized_layers: dict) -> "tuple[str, int, dict]":
    """Parse MIXED_PRECISION quantized_layers dict.

    Returns ``(dominant_quant_type, dominant_group_size, layer_overrides)``.
    ``layer_overrides`` maps **every** quantized module name to its quant-type
    string.  Modules not listed in ``quantized_layers`` are unquantized (FP16);
    ``make_linear`` falls back to FP16 when a module_name is absent from
    ``layer_overrides``.
    """
    from collections import Counter
    algo_count: Counter = Counter()
    algo_group_size: dict = {}
    for layer_cfg in quantized_layers.values():
        algo = layer_cfg.get("quant_algo", "").upper()
        algo_count[algo] += 1

        if algo not in algo_group_size:
            algo_group_size[algo] = int(layer_cfg.get("group_size", 1))
    if not algo_count:
        return QUANT_FP16, 1, {}
    dominant_algo = algo_count.most_common(1)[0][0]
    dominant_type = _algo_to_quant_type(dominant_algo)
    dominant_group_size = algo_group_size.get(dominant_algo, 1)
    # Expand fused projection keys (``self_attn.qkv_proj``,
    # ``mlp.gate_up_proj``) into the split names ``make_linear`` looks up
    # (``q_proj``/``k_proj``/``v_proj`` and ``gate_proj``/``up_proj``).
    layer_overrides: dict = {}
    for name, layer_cfg in quantized_layers.items():
        algo = layer_cfg.get("quant_algo", "").upper()
        short_name = _normalize_module_name(name)
        quant_type = _algo_to_quant_type(algo)
        if short_name.endswith(".self_attn.qkv_proj"):
            prefix = short_name[:-len("qkv_proj")]
            for proj in ("q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj"):
                layer_overrides[f"{prefix}{proj}"] = quant_type
        elif short_name.endswith(".mlp.gate_up_proj"):
            prefix = short_name[:-len("gate_up_proj")]
            for proj in ("gate_proj", "up_proj"):
                layer_overrides[f"{prefix}{proj}"] = quant_type
        else:
            layer_overrides[short_name] = quant_type
    return dominant_type, dominant_group_size, layer_overrides


def _kv_norm(s: Optional[str]) -> Optional[str]:
    if not s:
        return None
    return s.strip().lower() or None

128	# Visual submodule prefixes as they appear in HuggingFace VLM module paths.

131	# uses ``vision_model.*`` + ``mlp1.*``, Phi-4-multimodal nests the visual
132	# encoder under ``model.embed_tokens_extend.image_embed.*``, and Gemma4 uses
133	# ``embed_vision.*``.  This single tuple is the source of truth; everything
134	# below derives from it.
135	_VISUAL_PREFIXES = (

146	def _visual_quant_cfg(input_cfg: Optional[Dict[str, Any]],
147	                      weight_cfg: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]]:
148	    """Expand a single ``input_cfg`` / ``weight_cfg`` pair into wildcard
149	    patterns covering every visual prefix.
150	
151	    Modelopt matches patterns with ``fnmatch``; ``*visual*input_quantizer``
152	    only catches paths that contain the literal substring ``visual``, so a
153	    single-prefix pattern would silently leave ``vision_tower.*``,
154	    ``vision_model.*``, ``multi_modal_projector.*``, ``mlp1.*`` modules
155	    unquantized.  Emit one entry per prefix so all VLM families are covered.
156	
157	    ``input_cfg=None`` skips the input-quantizer entries (used for INT4 AWQ
158	    which is W4A16, weight-only).
159	    """
160	    out: Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]] = {}
161	    for prefix in _VISUAL_PREFIXES:
162	        out[f"*{prefix}*weight_quantizer"] = weight_cfg
163	        if input_cfg is not None:
164	            out[f"*{prefix}*input_quantizer"] = input_cfg
165	    return out

175	    "quant_cfg":

255	# ``q_bmm_quantizer`` attribute, not ``*_quantizer/input`` sub-selectors.

259	_CODE2WAV_PATTERNS = ("*code2wav.*", )

262	def _cp_quant_cfg(input_cfg: Optional[Dict[str, Any]],
263	                  weight_cfg: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]]:
264	    """Expand ``input_cfg`` / ``weight_cfg`` into CP wildcard patterns and
265	    layer disables for :data:`_CP_LINEAR_EXCLUDES` / :data:`_CP_BMM_EXCLUDES`.
266	
267	    Mirrors :func:`_visual_quant_cfg` / :func:`_audio_quant_cfg`; disables are
268	    emitted after generic enables so specific patterns win (ModelOpt matches
269	    in insertion order — last-writer wins).
270	    """
271	    out: Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]] = {}
272	    for prefix in _CP_PREFIXES:
273	        out[f"*{prefix}*weight_quantizer"] = weight_cfg
274	        if input_cfg is not None:
275	            out[f"*{prefix}*input_quantizer"] = input_cfg
276	    for prefix in _CP_PREFIXES:
277	        for sub in _CP_LINEAR_EXCLUDES:
278	            out[f"*{prefix}*{sub}*weight_quantizer"] = {"enable": False}
279	            out[f"*{prefix}*{sub}*input_quantizer"] = {"enable": False}
280	        for sub in _CP_BMM_EXCLUDES:
281	            out[f"*{prefix}*{sub}_quantizer"] = {"enable": False}
282	    return out
283	
284	
285	# CP FP8 recipe. Per-channel weight (axis=0) + per-tensor static input
286	# (axis=None); see :data:`_CP_SUBMODULE_EXCLUDES` for disabled submodules.
287	FP8_CP = {
288	    "quant_cfg":
289	    _cp_quant_cfg(
290	        input_cfg={
291	            "num_bits": (4, 3),
292	            "axis": None,
293	            "enable": True,
294	        },
295	        weight_cfg={
296	            "num_bits": (4, 3),
297	            "axis": 0,
298	            "enable": True,
299	        },
300	    )
301	}
302	
303	_BACKBONE_CFG_MAP = {
304	    "fp8": mtq.FP8_DEFAULT_CFG,
305	    "int4_awq": mtq.INT4_AWQ_CFG,
306	    "nvfp4": mtq.NVFP4_DEFAULT_CFG,
307	    "mxfp8": mtq.MXFP8_DEFAULT_CFG,
308	    "int8_sq": mtq.INT8_SMOOTHQUANT_CFG,
309	}
310	
311	_LM_HEAD_CFG_MAP = {
312	    "fp8": FP8_LM_HEAD,
313	    "int4_awq": INT4_AWQ_LM_HEAD,
314	    "nvfp4": NVFP4_LM_HEAD,
315	    "mxfp8": MXFP8_LM_HEAD,
316	}
317	
318	_VISUAL_CFG_MAP = {
319	    "fp8": FP8_VISUAL,
320	}
321	
322	_AUDIO_CFG_MAP = {
323	    "fp8": FP8_AUDIO,
324	}
325	
326	_CP_CFG_MAP = {
327	    "fp8": FP8_CP,
328	}
329	
330	
331	def _disable_groups(*pattern_groups) -> Dict[str, Dict[str, bool]]:
332	    """Flatten wildcard pattern groups into a ``{pattern: {enable: False}}`` dict."""
333	    out: Dict[str, Dict[str, bool]] = {}
334	    for group in pattern_groups:
335	        for k in group:
336	            out[k] = {"enable": False}
337	    return out
338	
339	
340	def _dict_entry_to_list_entry(pattern: str,
341	                              value: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
342	    """Convert legacy dict-style quant cfg entry to ModelOpt list-style entry."""
343	    entry: Dict[str, Any] = {
344	        "quantizer_name": "*" if pattern == "default" else pattern
345	    }
346	    if set(value.keys()) == {"enable"}:
347	        entry["enable"] = value["enable"]
348	        return entry
349	
350	    cfg_payload = {k: v for k, v in value.items() if k != "enable"}
351	    if cfg_payload:
352	        entry["cfg"] = cfg_payload
353	    if "enable" in value:
354	        entry["enable"] = value["enable"]
355	    return entry
356	
357	
358	def _merge_quant_cfg(target: Any, extra: Any) -> Any:
359	    """Merge quant cfg data while supporting both dict and list structures."""
360	    if isinstance(target, dict):
361	        if isinstance(extra, dict):
362	            target.update(extra)
363	            return target
364	        if isinstance(extra, list):
365	            for item in extra:
366	                if not isinstance(item, dict):
367	                    continue
368	                quantizer_name = item.get("quantizer_name")
369	                if not quantizer_name:
370	                    continue
371	                pattern = "default" if quantizer_name == "*" else quantizer_name
372	                merged_value: Dict[str, Any] = {}
373	                if isinstance(item.get("cfg"), dict):
374	                    cfg_payload = item["cfg"]
375	                    if "enable" in cfg_payload:
376	                        raise ValueError(
377	                            "Cannot losslessly convert ModelOpt list-style "
378	                            "quant_cfg to dict form when cfg contains "
379	                            "'enable'.")
380	                    merged_value.update(cfg_payload)
381	                if "enable" in item:
382	                    merged_value["enable"] = item["enable"]
383	                target[pattern] = merged_value
384	            return target
385	        raise TypeError(
386	            f"Unsupported quant_cfg source type for dict target: {type(extra)}"
387	        )
388	
389	    if isinstance(target, list):
390	        pending_entries: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
391	        if isinstance(extra, dict):
392	            for pattern, value in extra.items():
393	                if isinstance(value, dict):
394	                    pending_entries.append(
395	                        _dict_entry_to_list_entry(pattern, value))
396	        elif isinstance(extra, list):
397	            for item in extra:
398	                if isinstance(item, dict) and item.get("quantizer_name"):
399	                    pending_entries.append(copy.deepcopy(item))
400	        else:
401	            raise TypeError(
402	                f"Unsupported quant_cfg source type for list target: {type(extra)}"
403	            )
404	
405	        index_by_name = {}
406	        for idx, item in enumerate(target):
407	            if isinstance(item, dict) and item.get("quantizer_name"):
408	                index_by_name[item["quantizer_name"]] = idx
409	
410	        for entry in pending_entries:
411	            quantizer_name = entry["quantizer_name"]
412	            if quantizer_name in index_by_name:
413	                target[index_by_name[quantizer_name]] = entry
414	            else:
415	                index_by_name[quantizer_name] = len(target)
416	                target.append(entry)
417	        return target
418	
419	    raise TypeError(f"Unsupported quant_cfg target type: {type(target)}")
420	
421	
422	def _remove_lm_head_quantizers(quant_cfg: Any) -> Any:
423	    """Remove lm_head-related quantizers from dict/list quant cfg."""
424	    if isinstance(quant_cfg, dict):
425	        return {k: v for k, v in quant_cfg.items() if "lm_head" not in k}
426	    if isinstance(quant_cfg, list):
427	        return [
428	            item for item in quant_cfg
429	            if not ("lm_head" in str(item.get("quantizer_name", "")))
430	        ]
431	    raise TypeError(
432	        f"Unsupported quant_cfg type when removing lm_head: {type(quant_cfg)}")
433	
434	
435	def build_quant_config(
436	    quantization: Optional[str] = None,
437	    lm_head_quantization: Optional[str] = None,
438	    kv_cache_quantization: Optional[str] = None,
439	    visual_quantization: Optional[str] = None,
440	    audio_quantization: Optional[str] = None,
441	    cp_quantization: Optional[str] = None,
442	) -> Dict[str, Any]:
443	    """Build a composite ModelOpt quantization config from method names.
444	
445	    Args:
446	        quantization:          Backbone precision (``fp8`` / ``nvfp4`` / ...).
447	        lm_head_quantization:  Optional LM-head precision (typically matches
448	                               backbone; can also be lower for head sensitivity).
449	        kv_cache_quantization: Optional KV-cache precision (``fp8`` only).
450	        visual_quantization:   Optional visual-tower precision.  When ``None``
451	                               the visual tower is left untouched (``enable=False``
452	                               on all ``*visual.*`` / ``*vision_tower.*`` /
453	                               ``*multi_modal_projector.*`` / ``*mlp1.*``
454	                               patterns) — this preserves the existing behaviour.
455	                               When set to a method name the visual tower is
456	                               quantized via that method; if it matches the
457	                               backbone we just stop disabling, otherwise we
458	                               layer an explicit per-visual override on top.
459	        audio_quantization:    Optional audio-tower precision. Mirror of
460	                               ``visual_quantization`` for ``audio_tower.*`` /
461	                               ``audio_embed.*`` paths. Only ``fp8`` is exposed
462	                               today.
463	        cp_quantization:       Optional Qwen3-Omni CodePredictor precision
464	                               (``fp8`` only today).  When ``None`` the CP is
465	                               left untouched (``*code_predictor.*`` disabled);
466	                               when set, the CP backbone is quantized but
467	                               ``*code_predictor*down_proj*`` is explicitly
468	                               kept unquantized to preserve the FP32 MLP WAR.
469	    """
470	    if quantization is None:
471	        cfg = {"quant_cfg": {"default": {"enable": False}}, "algorithm": "max"}
472	    elif quantization in _BACKBONE_CFG_MAP:
473	        # Deep-copy so subsequent quant cfg merges do not mutate the shared
474	        # module-level dict/list that ModelOpt reuses for
475	        # every quant_algo (``mtq.NVFP4_DEFAULT_CFG`` and friends are
476	        # singletons; ``.copy()`` only copies the outer mapping, leaving
477	        # the inner ``quant_cfg`` aliased to the global).
478	        cfg = copy.deepcopy(_BACKBONE_CFG_MAP[quantization])
479	    else:
480	        raise ValueError(f"Unsupported quantization: {quantization}. "
481	                         f"Choose from: {list(_BACKBONE_CFG_MAP)}")
482	
483	    if lm_head_quantization is not None:
484	        if lm_head_quantization not in _LM_HEAD_CFG_MAP:
485	            raise ValueError(
486	                f"Unsupported lm_head_quantization: {lm_head_quantization}. "
487	                f"Choose from: {list(_LM_HEAD_CFG_MAP)}")
488	        cfg["quant_cfg"] = _remove_lm_head_quantizers(cfg["quant_cfg"])
489	        cfg["quant_cfg"] = _merge_quant_cfg(
490	            cfg["quant_cfg"],
491	            _LM_HEAD_CFG_MAP[lm_head_quantization]["quant_cfg"],
492	        )
493	
494	    if kv_cache_quantization == "fp8":
495	        cfg["quant_cfg"] = _merge_quant_cfg(cfg["quant_cfg"],
496	                                            mtq.FP8_KV_CFG["quant_cfg"])
497	        cfg["quant_cfg"] = _merge_quant_cfg(cfg["quant_cfg"],
498	                                            FP8_ATTN["quant_cfg"])
499	
500	    # Disable every non-LLM group by default. Re-enable the ones the user
501	    # explicitly asked to quantize.  ``_CODE2WAV_PATTERNS`` is always disabled
502	    # today; ``_CP_PATTERNS`` only when ``cp_quantization is None``.
503	    groups_to_disable = [_CODE2WAV_PATTERNS]
504	    if cp_quantization is None:
505	        groups_to_disable.append(_CP_PATTERNS)
506	    if visual_quantization is None:
507	        groups_to_disable.append(_VISUAL_PATTERNS)
508	    if audio_quantization is None:
509	        groups_to_disable.append(_AUDIO_PATTERNS)
510	    cfg["quant_cfg"] = _merge_quant_cfg(
511	        cfg["quant_cfg"],
512	        _disable_groups(*groups_to_disable),
513	    )
514	
515	    # CP override layered AFTER the generic disables so specific patterns win.
516	    if cp_quantization is not None:
517	        if cp_quantization not in _CP_CFG_MAP:
518	            raise ValueError(
519	                f"Unsupported cp_quantization: {cp_quantization}. "
520	                f"Choose from: {list(_CP_CFG_MAP)}")
521	        cfg["quant_cfg"] = _merge_quant_cfg(
522	            cfg["quant_cfg"],
523	            _CP_CFG_MAP[cp_quantization]["quant_cfg"],
524	        )
525	
526	    # When visual != backbone, layer an explicit override. When visual ==
527	    # backbone we don't need overrides — the backbone's generic wildcards
528	    # already cover visual submodules now that we've stopped disabling them.
529	    if (visual_quantization is not None
530	            and visual_quantization != quantization):
531	        if visual_quantization not in _VISUAL_CFG_MAP:
532	            raise ValueError(
533	                f"Unsupported visual_quantization: {visual_quantization}. "
534	                f"Choose from: {list(_VISUAL_CFG_MAP)}")
535	        cfg["quant_cfg"] = _merge_quant_cfg(
536	            cfg["quant_cfg"],
537	            _VISUAL_CFG_MAP[visual_quantization]["quant_cfg"],
538	        )
539	
540	    if (audio_quantization is not None and audio_quantization != quantization):
541	        if audio_quantization not in _AUDIO_CFG_MAP:
542	            raise ValueError(
543	                f"Unsupported audio_quantization: {audio_quantization}. "
544	                f"Choose from: {list(_AUDIO_CFG_MAP)}")
545	        cfg["quant_cfg"] = _merge_quant_cfg(
546	            cfg["quant_cfg"],
547	            _AUDIO_CFG_MAP[audio_quantization]["quant_cfg"],
548	        )
549	
550	    return cfg
551

15	"""Quantize a HuggingFace LLM and export a unified checkpoint.
16	
17	Loads a model via ``AutoModelForCausalLM`` (with ``AutoModelForImageTextToText``
18	fallback for VLMs), runs ModelOpt quantization, and writes a unified safetensors
19	checkpoint consumable by the checkpoint-based ``tensorrt_edgellm`` exporter.
20	"""
21	
22	import json
23	import os
24	import shutil
25	import time
26	from contextlib import contextmanager
27	from itertools import islice
28	from pathlib import Path
29	from typing import Any, Iterable, Optional, Union
30	
31	import modelopt.torch.quantization as mtq
32	import torch
33	from modelopt.torch.export import export_hf_checkpoint
34	from modelopt.torch.quantization.utils import is_quantized
35	from safetensors.torch import load_file
36	from torch.utils.data import DataLoader
37	from tqdm import tqdm
38	from transformers import (AutoModel, AutoModelForCausalLM,
39	                          AutoModelForImageTextToText,
40	                          AutoModelForTextToWaveform, AutoProcessor,
41	                          AutoTokenizer)
42	
43	from .datasets import (AudioDataset, ImageDataset, TextDataset, dataset_name,
44	                       resolve_dataset)
45	from .quantization_configs import build_quant_config
46	from .qwen3_asr_loader import (asr_calibration_dataloader, is_qwen3_asr_model,
47	                               load_qwen3_asr_joint_for_calibration,
48	                               postprocess_qwen3_asr_checkpoint)
49	from .qwen3_cp_loader import has_code_predictor, qwen3_cp_calibration_loop
50	
51	
52	def _text_calib_dataloader(tokenizer,
53	                           text_dataset: TextDataset,
54	                           batch_size=1,
55	                           num_samples=512,
56	                           max_length=512):
57	    """Return a DataLoader of tokenised ``input_ids`` for calibration.
58	
59	    ``text_dataset`` is a text generator function; the first ``num_samples``
60	    non-empty strings it yields are tokenized.
61	    """
62	    texts = list(islice(text_dataset(), num_samples))
63	    if not texts:
64	        raise ValueError(
65	            f"Text calibration dataset {dataset_name(text_dataset)!r} yielded "
66	            f"no samples. Check dataset access / fields.")
67	
68	    enc = tokenizer(texts,
69	                    return_tensors="pt",
70	                    padding=True,
71	                    truncation=True,
72	                    max_length=max_length)
73	    return DataLoader(enc["input_ids"], batch_size=batch_size, shuffle=False)
74	
75	
76	def _is_nemotron_h_model(model_dir: str) -> bool:
77	    """True if ``<model_dir>/config.json`` declares ``model_type == "nemotron_h"``."""
78	    config_path = os.path.join(model_dir, "config.json")
79	    if not os.path.exists(config_path):
80	        return False
81	    try:
82	        with open(config_path) as f:
83	            return json.load(f).get("model_type") == "nemotron_h"
84	    except (OSError, ValueError):
85	        return False
86	
87	
88	def _is_phi4mm_model(model_dir: str) -> bool:
89	    """True if ``<model_dir>/config.json`` declares a Phi-4MM checkpoint."""
90	    config_path = os.path.join(model_dir, "config.json")
91	    if not os.path.exists(config_path):
92	        return False
93	    try:
94	        with open(config_path) as f:
95	            model_type = json.load(f).get("model_type")
96	        return model_type in ("phi4mm", "phi4_multimodal")
97	    except (OSError, ValueError):
98	        return False
99	
100	
101	def _copy_phi4mm_processor_files(model_dir: str, output_dir: str) -> None:
102	    for name in ("preprocessor_config.json", "processor_config.json",
103	                 "processing_phi4mm.py"):
104	        src = os.path.join(model_dir, name)
105	        if os.path.exists(src):
106	            shutil.copy2(src, os.path.join(output_dir, name))
107	
108	
109	def _multimodal_calib_dataloader(processor,
110	                                 image_dataset: ImageDataset,
111	                                 num_samples: int = 128,
112	                                 max_length: int = 512,
113	                                 is_phi4mm: bool = False):
114	    """Yield ``BatchFeature`` dicts with ``input_ids`` + ``pixel_values``.
115	
116	    Streams ``(image, question)`` pairs from ``image_dataset`` through the
117	    model's own ``AutoProcessor`` chat template so the visual tower receives
118	    real activations.  Used when ``visual_quantization`` is set — text-only
119	    calibration would leave visual quantizers with uninitialised scales.
120	
121	    Drops down to 128 samples at batch_size=1 — VLM calibration is
122	    GPU-memory bound; small batches are safest.
123	    """
124	    batches: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
125	    for image, question in image_dataset():
126	        messages = [{
127	            "role":
128	            "user",
129	            "content": [
130	                {
131	                    "type": "image",
132	                    "image": image
133	                },
134	                {
135	                    "type": "text",
136	                    "text": question
137	                },
138	            ],
139	        }]
140	
141	        try:
142	            inputs = processor.apply_chat_template(
143	                messages,
144	                add_generation_prompt=True,
145	                tokenize=True,
146	                return_dict=True,
147	                return_tensors="pt",
148	            )
149	        except TypeError:
150	            if not is_phi4mm:
151	                raise
152	            # Phi-4MM's remote processor has a custom chat-template signature
153	            # that rejects ``tokenize`` / ``return_dict`` / ``return_tensors``.
154	            # It also uses textual image placeholders, so keep this fallback
155	            # Phi-4MM-only instead of applying ``<|image_1|>`` to other VLMs.
156	            fallback_messages = [{
157	                "role": "user",
158	                "content": f"<|image_1|>{question}",
159	            }]
160	            template_owner = processor if hasattr(
161	                processor, "apply_chat_template") else processor.tokenizer
162	            text = template_owner.apply_chat_template(
163	                fallback_messages, add_generation_prompt=True, tokenize=False)
164	            inputs = processor(text=text,
165	                               images=[image],
166	                               return_tensors="pt",
167	                               padding=True,
168	                               truncation=True,
169	                               max_length=max_length)
170	
171	        batches.append({
172	            k: v
173	            for k, v in inputs.items() if v is not None
174	            and not (isinstance(v, torch.Tensor) and v.numel() == 0)
175	        })
176	        if len(batches) >= num_samples:
177	            break
178	
179	    if not batches:
180	        raise RuntimeError(
181	            f"No usable multimodal samples from "
182	            f"{dataset_name(image_dataset)!r}. Check dataset access, fields, "
183	            "and the processor chat template.")
184	    return batches
185	
186	
187	def _load_model(model_dir, dtype="fp16", device="cuda"):
188	    """Load model + tokenizer + optional processor via Auto* classes."""
189	    torch_dtype = torch.float16 if dtype == "fp16" else torch.bfloat16
190	    tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(model_dir,
191	                                              trust_remote_code=True)
192	    try:
193	        processor = AutoProcessor.from_pretrained(model_dir,
194	                                                  trust_remote_code=True,
195	                                                  min_pixels=128 * 28 * 28,
196	                                                  max_pixels=2048 * 32 * 32)
197	    except Exception:
198	        processor = None
199	
200	    # NemotronH (hybrid Mamba+Attention): the custom modeling code imports
201	    # ``mamba_ssm.ops.triton.layernorm_gated`` and (when available)
202	    # ``causal_conv1d``. Apply the in-package patch BEFORE
203	    # AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained so the modeling import resolves
204	    # against pure-PyTorch substitutes. This mirrors the
205	    # tensorrt-edgellm-quantize flow, which applies the same patch before
206	    # model loading.
207	    if _is_phi4mm_model(model_dir):
208	        from tensorrt_edgellm.lora import load_phi4mm_model
209	        model = load_phi4mm_model(model_dir, torch_dtype)
210	        model.to(device)
211	    elif is_qwen3_asr_model(model_dir):
212	        # Qwen3-ASR HF ckpt declares model_type="qwen3_asr" but ships no
213	        # modeling code, so the AutoModel factories below would fail. We
214	        # build a *joint* calibration model: a vanilla Qwen3ForCausalLM
215	        # text decoder with the from-scratch Qwen3ASRAudioEncoder attached
216	        # as an ``audio_tower`` submodule plus a custom forward that
217	        # splices audio embeddings into the text input embedding stream
218	        # at <|audio_pad|> positions. ModelOpt's mtq.quantize walks the
219	        # joint module tree, so the same forward_loop drives both halves
220	        # under realistic ASR-shaped activations -- the equivalent of
221	        # _calibrate_multimodal for VLMs.
222	        model, tokenizer, processor = load_qwen3_asr_joint_for_calibration(
223	            model_dir, torch_dtype, device)
224	    else:
225	        if _is_nemotron_h_model(model_dir):
226	            from .nemotron_h_patch import apply as _apply_nemotron_h_patch
227	            _apply_nemotron_h_patch()
228	
229	        # Try ImageTextToText, then CausalLM, then the generic AutoModel.
230	        # ImageTextToText goes first because Qwen3.5 / Qwen3-VL register both
231	        # a CausalLM (text-only) and an ImageTextToText (multimodal)
232	        # architecture for the same checkpoint; AutoModelForCausalLM happily
233	        # resolves to the text-only entry and silently drops the visual tower
234	        # from the loaded model, breaking visual quantization downstream.
235	        # Some VLMs (e.g. InternVL3) are custom architectures registered only
236	        # under ``AutoModel``; the more specific factories raise
237	        # ``ValueError: Unrecognized configuration class``.  We only fall back
238	        # for *recognition* failures — not for ImportError or other runtime
239	        # errors, which would otherwise be silently masked by a misleading
240	        # "Unrecognized configuration class" exception.
241	        # Most-specific factory first: TextToWaveform > ImageTextToText >
242	        # CausalLM > AutoModel (fallback for custom architectures).
243	        factories = [
244	            f
245	            for f in (AutoModelForTextToWaveform, AutoModelForImageTextToText,
246	                      AutoModelForCausalLM, AutoModel) if f is not None
247	        ]
248	        last_err: Optional[Exception] = None
249	        for factory in factories:
250	            try:
251	                model = factory.from_pretrained(
252	                    model_dir,
253	                    torch_dtype=torch_dtype,
254	                    trust_remote_code=True,
255	                ).to(device)
256	                break
257	            except (ValueError, KeyError) as e:
258	                last_err = e
259	        else:
260	            raise RuntimeError(
261	                f"Could not load {model_dir} via any AutoModel factory"
262	            ) from last_err
263	
264	    model.to(torch_dtype)
265	
266	    # modelopt export_hf_checkpoint crashes when architectures is None
267	    # (e.g. Qwen3.5 resolves to text_config with architectures=None).
268	    if getattr(model.config, "architectures", None) is None:
269	        model.config.architectures = [type(model).__name__]
270	
271	    if tokenizer.pad_token is None:
272	        tokenizer.pad_token = tokenizer.eos_token
273	    return model, tokenizer, processor
274	
275	
276	def _collect_mtp_weight_map(
277	        weight_map: dict[str, str]) -> dict[str, list[str]]:
278	    """Group MTP tensor names by safetensors shard from an HF weight map."""
279	    mtp_weight_map: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
280	    for key, filename in weight_map.items():
281	        if "mtp" in key or "mtp" in filename:
282	            mtp_weight_map.setdefault(filename, []).append(key)
283	    return {
284	        filename: sorted(keys)
285	        for filename, keys in sorted(mtp_weight_map.items())
286	    }
287	
288	
289	def _extract_mtp_layer_prefixes(keys: Iterable[str]) -> list[str]:
290	    """Return MTP prefixes for the unquantized export fallback."""
291	    prefixes: set[str] = set()
292	    for key in keys:
293	        parts = key.split(".")
294	        if parts:
295	            prefixes.add(parts[0])
296	        for idx, part in enumerate(parts[:-1]):
297	            if part == "layers" and parts[idx + 1].isdigit():
298	                prefixes.add(".".join(parts[:idx + 2]))
299	                break
300	    return sorted(prefixes)
301	
302	
303	def _load_mtp_weights_for_unified_export(
304	        model: torch.nn.Module,
305	        model_dir: str) -> "tuple[list[str], dict[str, torch.Tensor]]":
306	    """Load unquantized Qwen3.5 MTP tensors for unified export fallback.
307	
308	    This mirrors ModelOpt's ``load_mtp_weights`` + ``export_hf_checkpoint``
309	    usage while keeping the checkpoint parsing deterministic and explicit:
310	    tensors already present on the loaded HF model are copied into the model
311	    state, and tensors absent from ``model.state_dict()`` are returned for
312	    ``export_hf_checkpoint(extra_state_dict=...)``.
313	    """
314	    ckpt_dir = Path(model_dir)
315	    index_path = ckpt_dir / "model.safetensors.index.json"
316	    if not index_path.exists():
317	        return [], {}
318	
319	    with index_path.open(encoding="utf-8") as f:
320	        weight_map = json.load(f).get("weight_map", {})
321	
322	    mtp_weight_map = _collect_mtp_weight_map(weight_map)
323	    if not mtp_weight_map:
324	        return [], {}
325	
326	    mtp_keys = [key for keys in mtp_weight_map.values() for key in keys]
327	    mtp_layer_prefixes = _extract_mtp_layer_prefixes(mtp_keys)
328	    model_state_keys = set(model.state_dict())
329	    extra_state_dict: dict[str, torch.Tensor] = {}
330	    loaded_count = 0
331	
332	    for filename, keys in mtp_weight_map.items():
333	        path = ckpt_dir / filename
334	        if not path.exists():
335	            raise FileNotFoundError(
336	                f"MTP shard referenced by index not found: "
337	                f"{path}")
338	        print(f"Loading {len(keys)} MTP tensor(s) from {filename}...")
339	        key_set = set(keys)
340	        tensors = load_file(str(path), device="cpu")
341	        tensors = {
342	            key: tensor
343	            for key, tensor in tensors.items() if key in key_set
344	        }
345	        in_state_dict = {
346	            key: tensor
347	            for key, tensor in tensors.items() if key in model_state_keys
348	        }
349	        if in_state_dict:
350	            model.load_state_dict(in_state_dict, strict=False)
351	            loaded_count += len(in_state_dict)
352	        extra_state_dict.update({
353	            key: tensor
354	            for key, tensor in tensors.items() if key not in model_state_keys
355	        })
356	
357	    if loaded_count or extra_state_dict:
358	        print("Loaded MTP tensors for unified export: "
359	              f"{loaded_count} in model state, "
360	              f"{len(extra_state_dict)} via extra_state_dict.")
361	    if mtp_layer_prefixes:
362	        print(f"Detected unquantized MTP prefixes for quantization ignore: "
363	              f"{mtp_layer_prefixes}")
364	
365	    return mtp_layer_prefixes, extra_state_dict
366

368	def _mtp_num_hidden_layers(model: torch.nn.Module) -> int:
369	    """Return the number of dense MTP draft layers declared by a HF config."""
370	    text_config = getattr(model.config, "text_config", model.config)
371	    return int(getattr(text_config, "mtp_num_hidden_layers", 0) or 0)

374	def _calibrate(model, dataloader):
375	    """Forward-loop calibration pass."""
376	    for data in tqdm(dataloader, desc="Calibrating"):
377	        data = data.to(model.device)
378	        if getattr(getattr(model, "config", None), "model_type",
379	                   "") in ("phi4mm", "phi4_multimodal"):
380	            model(input_ids=data, input_mode=0, use_cache=False)
381	        else:
382	            model(data)

384	
385	def _collect_attention_q_scales_for_export(
386	        model: torch.nn.Module) -> dict[str, torch.Tensor]:
387	    """Preserve calibrated FP8 Q-BMM scales in the exported checkpoint.
388	
389	    ModelOpt retains the calibrated Q-BMM amax on the quantizer, but checkpoint
390	    export does not emit it as ``q_proj.q_scale``. Edge-LLM needs that scale to
391	    quantize prefill Q to E4M3 without saturation.
392	    """
393	    q_scales: dict[str, torch.Tensor] = {}
394	    for module_name, module in model.named_modules():
395	        quantizer = getattr(module, "q_bmm_quantizer", None)
396	        if quantizer is None or not getattr(quantizer, "is_enabled", False):
397	            continue
398	        if getattr(module, "q_proj", None) is None:
399	            continue
400	
401	        amax = getattr(quantizer, "_amax", None)
402	        if amax is None:
403	            raise RuntimeError(
404	                f"Enabled Q-BMM quantizer {module_name}.q_bmm_quantizer "
405	                "has no calibrated amax")
406	        if amax.numel() != 1:
407	            raise RuntimeError(
408	                f"Q-BMM quantizer {module_name}.q_bmm_quantizer must use "
409	                f"a per-tensor scale, got shape {tuple(amax.shape)}")
410	
411	        maxbound = float(quantizer.maxbound)
412	        if maxbound <= 0.0:
413	            raise RuntimeError(
414	                f"Invalid Q-BMM maxbound for {module_name}: {maxbound}")
415	        scale = amax.detach().float().reshape(1).cpu() / maxbound
416	        if not torch.isfinite(scale).all() or scale.item() <= 0.0:
417	            raise RuntimeError(
418	                f"Invalid calibrated Q-BMM scale for {module_name}: "
419	                f"amax={amax.item()}, maxbound={maxbound}")
420	        prefix = f"{module_name}." if module_name else ""
421	        q_scales[f"{prefix}q_proj.q_scale"] = scale
422	
423	    return q_scales
424	
425	
426	def _normalize_tied_weights_keys(model) -> None:
427	    """WAR for transformers >= 5.x ``_tied_weights_keys`` format change.
428	
429	    Newer transformers expects each submodule's ``_tied_weights_keys``
430	    attribute to be a *dict-like* (so ``modeling_utils._get_tied_weight_keys``
431	    can call ``.keys()``). Older custom modeling code (e.g. NemotronH's
432	    ``modeling_nemotron_h.py``) still declares it as a *list*, which
433	    crashes ``model.save_pretrained`` with::
434	
435	        AttributeError: 'list' object has no attribute 'keys'
436	
437	    Convert list-shaped attributes to ``{key: key}`` dicts in place. The
438	    dict's keys exactly match the original list, preserving behavior for
439	    downstream tied-weight tracking. No-op for modules that already use
440	    the dict format or have no ``_tied_weights_keys`` set.
441	    """
442	    for module in model.modules():
443	        attr = getattr(module, "_tied_weights_keys", None)
444	        if isinstance(attr, list):
445	            module._tied_weights_keys = {k: k for k in attr}
446	
447	
448	def _remove_stale_safetensors_index(output_dir: str) -> None:
449	    """Remove a stale shard index when export produced a single safetensors file."""
450	    index_path = os.path.join(output_dir, "model.safetensors.index.json")
451	    single_path = os.path.join(output_dir, "model.safetensors")
452	    if not (os.path.exists(index_path) and os.path.exists(single_path)):
453	        return
454	    try:
455	        with open(index_path, encoding="utf-8") as f:
456	            weight_map = json.load(f).get("weight_map", {})
457	    except (OSError, json.JSONDecodeError):
458	        return
459	    if any(not os.path.exists(os.path.join(output_dir, shard))
460	           for shard in set(weight_map.values())):
461	        os.remove(index_path)
462	
463	
464	def _fix_generation_config_for_strict_validate(model) -> None:
465	    """WAR for transformers >= 5.x ``GenerationConfig.validate(strict=True)``.
466	
467	    ModelOpt's ``export_hf_checkpoint`` -> ``model.save_pretrained`` ->
468	    ``generation_config.save_pretrained`` runs ``validate(strict=True)``
469	    which rejects HF model checkpoints whose ``generation_config.json``
470	    sets sampling-only kwargs (``top_p`` / ``top_k`` / ``temperature``)
471	    without setting ``do_sample = True``. NVIDIA-Nemotron-3-Nano-* and
472	    similar checkpoints ship that exact mismatch.
473	
474	    Force ``do_sample = True`` when any sampling kwarg is present. This
475	    only changes the saved ``generation_config.json``; the C++ runtime
476	    (llm_inference / llm_bench) reads its own runtime params and does
477	    not depend on this file.
478	    """
479	    gc = getattr(model, "generation_config", None)
480	    if gc is None:
481	        return
482	    sampling_set = (getattr(gc, "top_p", None) not in (None, 1.0)
483	                    or getattr(gc, "top_k", None) not in (None, 0, 50)
484	                    or getattr(gc, "temperature", None) not in (None, 1.0))
485	    if sampling_set and not getattr(gc, "do_sample", False):
486	        gc.do_sample = True
487	
488	
489	def _is_hybrid_model(model):
490	    """Return True if the model has hybrid Mamba+Attention layers.
491	
492	    Checks multiple signals: ``layers_block_type`` in config (NemotronH),
493	    ``mamba_ssm_dtype`` in config (Qwen3.5), or ``linear_attn`` submodules.
494	    """
495	    config = model.config
496	    if hasattr(config, "text_config"):
497	        config = config.text_config
498	    if getattr(config, "layers_block_type", None) is not None:
499	        return True
500	    if getattr(config, "mamba_ssm_dtype", None) is not None:
501	        return True
502	    if any("linear_attn" in n for n, _ in model.named_modules()):
503	        return True
504	    return False
505	
506	
507	@contextmanager
508	def _skip_resmooth_for_hybrid(model, quantization: str = ""):
509	    """WAR for ModelOpt resmoothing bugs on selected custom models.
510	
511	    ``export_hf_checkpoint`` calls ``requantize_resmooth_fused_llm_layers``
512	    which averages AWQ pre_quant_scales across all linear modules that share
513	    the same input and re-quantizes their weights.  For hybrid models the
514	    dummy forward used to detect shared inputs does not propagate through
515	    Mamba layers correctly, and the Mamba projections (qkv, z, a, b) get
516	    incorrectly fused, corrupting the int4 weights.  For Phi-4 multimodal,
517	    the dummy forward is incompatible with the required ``input_mode``.
518	
519	    NVFP4 is exempt: resmoothing works correctly for NVFP4 and is required
520	    to equalise per-tensor scales across GDN input projections that share
521	    the same input activation, enabling fusion into a single GEMM.
522	
523	    This context manager patches the resmoothing function to a no-op when the
524	    model needs it.  Standard transformer models are unaffected.
525	
526	    TODO: Remove once ModelOpt fixes these model paths upstream.
527	    """
528	    model_type = getattr(getattr(model, "config", None), "model_type", "")
529	    # NVFP4 on hybrid models: resmoothing is safe and required for GDN
530	    # input projection fusion — do NOT skip.
531	    is_nvfp4 = quantization.lower() in ("nvfp4", "fp4")
532	    # Multimodal wrappers have no top-level ``forward``; resmooth's dummy
533	    # ``model(fake_input)`` crashes on them. Resmooth is a no-op without
534	    # AWQ pre_quant_scales, so skipping is safe here.
535	    should_skip = ((_is_hybrid_model(model) and not is_nvfp4)
536	                   or model_type in ("phi4mm", "phi4_multimodal", "qwen3_omni",
537	                                     "qwen3_omni_moe", "qwen3_omni_next"))
538	    if not should_skip:
539	        yield
540	        return
541	
542	    import modelopt.torch.export.unified_export_hf as _ueh
543	    _orig = _ueh.requantize_resmooth_fused_llm_layers
544	
545	    def _noop(m):
546	        print("[WAR] Skipping requantize_resmooth_fused_llm_layers "
547	              "for this model (ModelOpt bug workaround)")
548	
549	    _ueh.requantize_resmooth_fused_llm_layers = _noop
550	    try:
551	        yield
552	    finally:
553	        _ueh.requantize_resmooth_fused_llm_layers = _orig
554	
555	
556	def _calibrate_multimodal(model, batches):
557	    """Forward-loop calibration pass for multimodal ``BatchFeature`` dicts."""
558	    device = model.device
559	    valid_batches = 0
560	    skipped_nan_batches = 0
561	    for batch in tqdm(batches, desc="Calibrating (multimodal)"):
562	        kwargs = {}
563	        for k, v in batch.items():
564	            if isinstance(v, torch.Tensor):
565	                v = v.to(device)
566	                # Float inputs (pixel_values) inherit the model's dtype;
567	                # int inputs (input_ids, attention_mask) stay untouched.
568	                if v.dtype.is_floating_point:
569	                    v = v.to(next(model.parameters()).dtype)
570	            kwargs[k] = v
571	        kwargs.setdefault("use_cache", False)
572	        with torch.no_grad():
573	            try:
574	                model(**kwargs)
575	                valid_batches += 1
576	            except AssertionError as exc:
577	                # Some multimodal samples can produce non-finite activations
578	                # during calibrator collection. ModelOpt 0.44 reports this
579	                # through an internal assert on the calibrator amax value; skip
580	                # those samples so calibration can complete with valid batches.
581	                if "detected nan values in amax" in str(exc):
582	                    skipped_nan_batches += 1
583	                    continue
584	                raise
585	    if valid_batches == 0:
586	        raise RuntimeError(
587	            "All multimodal calibration batches were skipped due to NaN amax.")
588	    if skipped_nan_batches > 0:
589	        print(
590	            f"[WAR] Skipped {skipped_nan_batches} multimodal calibration batch(es) with NaN amax."
591	        )
592	
593	
594	def _calibrate_asr_multimodal(model, batch_iter):
595	    """Forward-loop calibration pass for joint ASR (audio + text) batches.
596	
597	    Drives the joint Qwen3-ASR calibration model -- :func:`Qwen3ASR
598	    audio_tower` + vanilla ``Qwen3ForCausalLM`` text decoder + audio splice
599	    -- with real (audio, transcript) pairs so quantizers on both the audio
600	    and text paths see realistic activations. Mirror of
601	    :func:`_calibrate_multimodal` but consumes a generator (the LibriSpeech
602	    streaming dataloader is finite-but-streamed, not pre-materialised).
603	    """
604	    device = model.device
605	    model_dtype = next(model.parameters()).dtype
606	    for batch in tqdm(batch_iter, desc="Calibrating (ASR multimodal)"):
607	        kwargs = {}
608	        for k, v in batch.items():
609	            if isinstance(v, torch.Tensor):
610	                v = v.to(device)
611	                if v.dtype.is_floating_point:
612	                    v = v.to(model_dtype)
613	            kwargs[k] = v
614	        with torch.no_grad():
615	            model(**kwargs)
616	
617	
618	def quantize_and_export(
619	    model_dir: str,
620	    output_dir: str,
621	    quantization: Optional[str] = None,
622	    lm_head_quantization: Optional[str] = None,
623	    visual_quantization: Optional[str] = None,
624	    cp_quantization: Optional[str] = None,
625	    kv_cache_quantization: Optional[str] = None,
626	    audio_quantization: Optional[str] = None,
627	    dtype: str = "fp16",
628	    device: str = "cuda",
629	    *,
630	    text_dataset: Union[str, TextDataset, None] = None,
631	    image_dataset: Union[str, ImageDataset, None] = None,
632	    audio_dataset: Union[str, AudioDataset, None] = None,
633	    num_samples: int = 512,
634	) -> str:
635	    """Load a HuggingFace model, quantize it, and export a unified checkpoint.
636	
637	    ``visual_quantization`` turns on quantization of the visual tower
638	    (``visual.*`` / ``vision_tower.*`` / ``multi_modal_projector.*``); when
639	    ``None`` (default) the visual tower stays in fp16.  Quantizing the visual
640	    tower with text-only calibration produces uninitialised activation scales
641	    on the visual path — a multimodal calibration loader is required for
642	    accurate visual stats (see ``A3``).
643	
644	    ``text_dataset`` / ``image_dataset`` / ``audio_dataset`` each accept a
645	    registered dataset name (str), a dataset generator function, or ``None``
646	    for that modality's default. Only the dataset for the modality a run
647	    actually calibrates is resolved, so an unknown name for an unused modality
648	    never fails the run; an unknown name for the modality in use fails out
649	    with a pointer to the customization guide.
650	    """
651	    t0 = time.time()
652	    model, tokenizer, processor = _load_model(model_dir, dtype, device)
653	
654	    mtp_layers = _mtp_num_hidden_layers(model)
655	    mtp_quantized = False
656	    mtp_state_dict: dict[str, torch.Tensor] = {}
657	    if (mtp_layers > 0 and quantization is not None
658	            and not is_quantized(model)):
659	        text_ds = resolve_dataset(text_dataset, "text")
660	        from .models.mtp_draft import (export_quantized_mtp_state_dict,
661	                                       quantize_mtp_from_base)
662	        print(f"Detected {mtp_layers} MTP layer(s); quantizing MTP draft "
663	              "before base model.")
664	        quantized_mtp_draft = quantize_mtp_from_base(
665	            base_model=model,
666	            tokenizer=tokenizer,
667	            model_dir=model_dir,
668	            quantization=quantization,
669	            lm_head_quantization=lm_head_quantization,
670	            kv_cache_quantization=kv_cache_quantization,
671	            dtype=dtype,
672	            device=device,
673	            text_dataset=text_ds,
674	            num_samples=num_samples,
675	        )
676	        mtp_state_dict = export_quantized_mtp_state_dict(
677	            quantized_mtp_draft, dtype)
678	        print(f"Prepared {len(mtp_state_dict)} quantized MTP tensor(s) for "
679	              "unified export.")
680	        mtp_quantized = True
681	        del quantized_mtp_draft
682	        if torch.cuda.is_available():
683	            torch.cuda.empty_cache()
684	
685	    # --- Quantize base model ----------------------------------------------
686	    if is_quantized(model):
687	        print("Model already quantized — skipping.")
688	    else:
689	        # Fail fast when the user asks for CP quantization on a model that
690	        # has no CodePredictor — otherwise the cp_quantization argument
691	        # silently no-ops (build_quant_config still adds *code_predictor*
692	        # wildcards but they match nothing).
693	        if cp_quantization is not None and not has_code_predictor(model):
694	            raise ValueError(
695	                f"--cp_quantization={cp_quantization} requires a model with "
696	                "talker.code_predictor (Qwen3-Omni / Qwen3-TTS); the loaded "
697	                "checkpoint has none.")
698	        # MoE Thinker backbone quantization runs through the dedicated
699	        # ``tensorrt-edgellm-quantize thinker`` command (qwen3_omni_thinker.py);
700	        # the ``llm`` command can't dummy-walk the MoE wrapper. Joint mode
701	        # here would silently produce a Thinker-unquantized checkpoint.
702	        if (cp_quantization is not None and quantization is not None
703	                and getattr(model, "thinker", None) is not None
704	                and "Moe" in type(model).__name__):
705	            raise ValueError(
706	                "Joint --quantization + --cp_quantization on the Qwen3-Omni-MoE "
707	                "wrapper is not supported here. Use `--cp_quantization fp8` "
708	                "alone via `tensorrt-edgellm-quantize llm`, and run "
709	                "`tensorrt-edgellm-quantize thinker --quantization fp8` "
710	                "separately for the MoE Thinker backbone.")
711	        quant_cfg = build_quant_config(
712	            quantization,
713	            lm_head_quantization,
714	            kv_cache_quantization,
715	            visual_quantization=visual_quantization,
716	            audio_quantization=audio_quantization,
717	            cp_quantization=cp_quantization,
718	        )
719	        if cp_quantization is not None and has_code_predictor(model):
720	            # CP is only reached via the Thinker->Talker->CP generation path,
721	            # so a dedicated loop drives that chain (bs=1: Talker uses 3D
722	            # RoPE, no batch-mixing). When backbone is co-quantized, prepend
723	            # a standard text pass; backbone forward doesn't fire CP
724	            # quantizers so CP amax matches standalone mode.
725	            text_ds = resolve_dataset(text_dataset, "text")
726	            print(f"Text calibration dataset: {dataset_name(text_ds)}")
727	            cp_loader = _text_calib_dataloader(tokenizer,
728	                                               text_ds,
729	                                               batch_size=1,
730	                                               num_samples=num_samples)
731	            cp_n = min(num_samples, 64)
732	            if quantization is not None:
733	                bb_loader = _text_calib_dataloader(tokenizer,
734	                                                   text_ds,
735	                                                   batch_size=16,
736	                                                   num_samples=num_samples)
737	
738	                def _joint_cp_loop(m):
739	                    _calibrate(m, bb_loader)
740	                    qwen3_cp_calibration_loop(m,
741	                                              cp_loader,
742	                                              num_cp_samples=cp_n)
743	
744	                mtq.quantize(model, quant_cfg, forward_loop=_joint_cp_loop)
745	            else:
746	                mtq.quantize(
747	                    model,
748	                    quant_cfg,
749	                    forward_loop=lambda m: qwen3_cp_calibration_loop(
750	                        m, cp_loader, num_cp_samples=cp_n),
751	                )
752	        elif is_qwen3_asr_model(model_dir):
753	            audio_ds = resolve_dataset(audio_dataset, "audio")
754	            print(f"Audio calibration dataset: {dataset_name(audio_ds)}")
755	            # ASR multimodal calibration: stream real (audio, transcript)
756	            # pairs through the joint audio_tower + text decoder so the
757	            # text quantizers see audio-embedding-spliced inputs (the
758	            # distribution they actually see at runtime). Mirror of the
759	            # visual_quantization branch below for VLMs.
760	            asr_samples = min(num_samples, 128)
761	            audio_n_window = int(model.audio_tower.config.get("n_window", 100))
762	            num_mel_bins = int(
763	                model.audio_tower.config.get("num_mel_bins", 128))
764	            audio_token_id = int(model._asr_audio_token_id)
765	            # Materialize the generator so ModelOpt can re-iterate
766	            # forward_loop (e.g. AutoQuantize algorithm selection).
767	            # Mirrors the visual path's _multimodal_calib_dataloader,
768	            # which returns a list for the same reason.
769	            batches = list(
770	                asr_calibration_dataloader(
771	                    tokenizer=tokenizer,
772	                    audio_token_id=audio_token_id,
773	                    audio_n_window=audio_n_window,
774	                    num_mel_bins=num_mel_bins,
775	                    audio_dataset=audio_ds,
776	                    num_samples=asr_samples,
777	                ))
778	            mtq.quantize(
779	                model,
780	                quant_cfg,
781	                forward_loop=lambda m: _calibrate_asr_multimodal(m, batches),
782	            )
783	        elif visual_quantization is not None:
784	            image_ds = resolve_dataset(image_dataset, "image")
785	            print(f"Image calibration dataset: {dataset_name(image_ds)}")
786	            # Multimodal calibration: feed (image, text) pairs through the
787	            # whole VLM so visual + LLM quantizers both see real activations.
788	            processor = AutoProcessor.from_pretrained(model_dir,
789	                                                      trust_remote_code=True)
790	            mm_samples = min(num_samples, 128)
791	            batches = _multimodal_calib_dataloader(
792	                processor,
793	                image_dataset=image_ds,
794	                num_samples=mm_samples,
795	                is_phi4mm=_is_phi4mm_model(model_dir))
796	            mtq.quantize(
797	                model,
798	                quant_cfg,
799	                forward_loop=lambda m: _calibrate_multimodal(m, batches),
800	            )
801	        else:
802	            text_ds = resolve_dataset(text_dataset, "text")
803	            print(f"Text calibration dataset: {dataset_name(text_ds)}")
804	            batch_size = 16 if quantization in (None, "int4_awq") else 1
805	            loader = _text_calib_dataloader(tokenizer,
806	                                            text_ds,
807	                                            batch_size=batch_size,
808	                                            num_samples=num_samples)
809	            mtq.quantize(model,
810	                         quant_cfg,
811	                         forward_loop=lambda m: _calibrate(m, loader))
812	        mtq.print_quant_summary(model)
813	
814	    print(f"Quantization: {time.time() - t0:.1f}s")
815	
816	    _fix_generation_config_for_strict_validate(model)
817	    _normalize_tied_weights_keys(model)
818	
819	    if mtp_layers > 0 and not mtp_quantized:
820	        mtp_layer_prefixes, mtp_state_dict = (
821	            _load_mtp_weights_for_unified_export(model, model_dir))
822	        if mtp_layer_prefixes:
823	            model._mtp_layer_prefixes = mtp_layer_prefixes
824	
825	    attention_q_scales = _collect_attention_q_scales_for_export(model)
826	    extra_state_dict = dict(mtp_state_dict)
827	    extra_state_dict.update(attention_q_scales)
828	
829	    os.makedirs(output_dir, exist_ok=True)
830	    # MoE wrapper has no top-level ``forward`` → ``export_hf_checkpoint``'s
831	    # dummy walk crashes. Route CP-only quantization on such wrappers through
832	    # ``qwen3_omni._export_submodel(model, "talker", ...)``.
833	    cp_only_moe_wrapper = (cp_quantization is not None and quantization is None
834	                           and has_code_predictor(model)
835	                           and getattr(model, "thinker", None) is not None
836	                           and "Moe" in type(model).__name__)
837	    if cp_only_moe_wrapper:
838	        from .qwen3_omni import _export_submodel
839	        _export_submodel(model, "talker", output_dir)
840	    else:
841	        with torch.inference_mode(), _skip_resmooth_for_hybrid(
842	                model, quantization or ""):
843	            export_hf_checkpoint(model,
844	                                 export_dir=output_dir,
845	                                 extra_state_dict=extra_state_dict)
846	        if attention_q_scales:
847	            print("Exported calibrated Q-BMM scales for "
848	                  f"{len(attention_q_scales)} attention layer(s).")
849	    _remove_stale_safetensors_index(output_dir)
850	    tokenizer.save_pretrained(output_dir)
851	    if processor is not None:
852	        if _is_phi4mm_model(model_dir):
853	            _copy_phi4mm_processor_files(model_dir, output_dir)
854	        else:
855	            processor.save_pretrained(output_dir)
856	
857	    # Copy preprocessor / processor configs so downstream tools (tensorrt_edgellm's
858	    # tensorrt-edgellm-export, the C++ visual builder) can find image preprocessing
859	    # parameters (patch_size, image_mean, image_std, ...).  ``export_hf_checkpoint``
860	    # only writes the model + hf_quant_config; processor metadata is part of the
861	    # source HF directory and must be carried over explicitly.
862	    for fname in ("preprocessor_config.json", "processor_config.json",
863	                  "video_preprocessor_config.json", "chat_template.jinja"):
864	        src = os.path.join(model_dir, fname)
865	        if os.path.isfile(src):
866	            shutil.copy2(src, os.path.join(output_dir, fname))
867	
868	    # Qwen3-ASR: convert the vanilla-Qwen3-shaped output back into the
869	    # qwen3_asr layout the runtime expects (re-prefix safetensors keys with
870	    # ``thinker.``, restore the qwen3_asr config.json + chat_template +
871	    # preprocessor). ``audio_tower.*`` weights are already in the exported
872	    # safetensors -- the joint calibration model carries them as a submodule.
873	    if is_qwen3_asr_model(model_dir):
874	        postprocess_qwen3_asr_checkpoint(model_dir, output_dir)
875	
876	    print(f"Saved to {output_dir} (total {time.time() - t0:.1f}s)")
877	    return output_dir
878

# SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Copyright (c) 2025-2026 NVIDIA CORPORATION & AFFILIATES. All rights reserved.
# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0

#
